channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ramaphosa and Lesotho leadership to inaugurate Senqu Bridge under Phase II of Lesotho Highlands Water Project
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa is set to travel to Lesotho this week to take part in the official opening of the Senqu Bridge in Mokhotlong, alongside King Letsie III and Prime Minister Samuel Matekane. Th...

item.title: NYSC releases 2026 Batch A Stream II call-up letters; orientation begins April 22
item.description: The National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) has officially released call-up letters for prospective corps members in the 2026 Batch ‘A’ Stream II, marking a key step ahead of the mandatory national servic...

item.title: Four arrested after disorder breaks out at Epsom protest linked to rape investigation
item.description: Four people have been arrested following a protest in Epsom, Surrey, which escalated into public disorder amid an ongoing police investigation into a reported rape. According to Surrey Police, the dem...

item.title: 81-year-old Catherine Mount continues lifelong passion for skydiving
item.description: Catherine Mount, an 81-year-old skydiving enthusiast, is challenging common assumptions about age and physical limits through her continued participation in the high-adrenaline sport. Featured in a li...

item.title: Second tunnel-boring machine marks major progress in Lesotho Highlands Water Project Phase II
item.description: Government leaders from South Africa and Lesotho are set to unveil a second advanced tunnel-boring machine (TBM) at the Polihali construction site in Mokhotlong, marking a key milestone in Phase II of...

item.title: Sheep shearing competition proposal in Western Australia surprises partner
item.description: A Western Australian man named Jayden has surprised his partner with an unexpected marriage proposal during a sheep shearing competition, in what onlookers have described as a very distinctly Australi...

item.title: Nigerian Telecom Regulators Collaborate on Cybersecurity Enhancement
item.description: The Nigerian Communications Commission (NCC) and the National Information Technology Development Agency (NITDA) have announced a collaborative effort to strengthen cybersecurity measures within the co...

item.title: Ex‑hate‑crimes official suing Eric Adams takes top post in Mamdani administration
item.description: Hassan Naveed, the former head of New York City’s hate‑crimes prevention office who was fired in 2024 and is now suing the city and former Mayor Eric Adams for alleged religious discrimination, has re...

item.title: Mental health care in Burkina Faso: cultural beliefs, staff shortages and fragile systems
item.description: Burkina Faso’s mental health system faces a tough mix of cultural, structural and resource challenges. In a country of more than 20 million people, there are just over 100 mental health professionals,...

item.title: Agritourism expands across Alberta as farms open doors to visitors
item.description: Agritourism is rapidly expanding across Alberta as more farms open their operations to visitors seeking authentic experiences and a better understanding of where their food comes from. The trend refle...

item.title: Paint warehouse in Valenzuela catches fire, authorities respond
item.description: A paint warehouse in Valenzuela City caught fire early Tuesday morning, April 21, 2026, as reported by ABS-CBN News. The incident involved a storage facility containing paint materials located in Bara...

item.title: Starmer denies misleading Parliament over Mandelson’s vetting while facing MPs’ scrutiny
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has insisted he did not mislead the House of Commons after fresh questions emerged over the vetting of Lord Peter Mandelson for his role as UK ambassador to the United Stat...

item.title: King Charles commemorates Queen Elizabeth II’s centenary with tribute and memorial plans
item.description: King Charles III has marked what would have been Queen Elizabeth II’s 100th birthday with a personal video tribute, reflecting on her lifelong commitment to public service and her enduring legacy. Spe...

item.title: Alphonce Simbu Represents Tanzania in Competitive Boston Marathon Field
item.description: Tanzania’s long-distance runner Alphonce Simbu is taking part in the 2026 Boston Marathon, one of the world’s most respected and historic races. The event, now in its 129th edition, brings together el...

item.title: Latricia Ian wins Miss World Tanzania 2026 title and two major awards
item.description: Dar es Salaam witnessed a historic beauty pageant as Latricia Ian emerged the first-ever Miss World Tanzania during an event held at The Super Dome on April 20, 2026. The competition brought together ...

item.title: Security forces kill key militant linked to February attack in Bannu operation
item.description: Security forces carried out an intelligence-based operation (IBO) in Bannu district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, resulting in the killing of two militants, including a key figure linked to a February suicid...

item.title: Northeastern Ontario housing market sees decline in sales and prices
item.description: The housing market in Northeastern Ontario experienced a slowdown last month, with both home sales and prices declining, according to the report. The article highlights a cooling trend in the regional...

item.title: PM Modi’s jhalmuri stop during Bengal campaign sparks viral interest and political reactions
item.description: The humble street snack jhalmuri has suddenly come into the national spotlight after Prime Minister Narendra Modi was seen enjoying it during a campaign visit in West Bengal’s Jhargram. The incident t...

item.title: First Lady Liza Marcos warns public against impersonation scams using her name
item.description: First Lady Liza Marcos has warned the public about scammers who are using her name to deceive individuals and attempt to solicit money through fraudulent messages. In a Facebook post, she clarified th...

item.title: Strong 7.4 Magnitude Earthquake Triggers 80cm Tsunami in Northern Japan, Evacuation Warnings Issued
item.description: A powerful 7.4-magnitude earthquake struck northern Japan on Monday, triggering tsunami waves and prompting emergency evacuation warnings along coastal regions. According to Japan’s Meteorological Age...
